findings.search_query: Sarah Barker 1816 New Brunswick John Holland Bridges genealogy Maine
findings.source_title: Sarah Bridges (Thorncroft) (1816 - 1889) - Geni
findings.snippet: Sarah Bridges (Thorncroft) was born in 1816 in New Brunswick, Canada and died in 1889 in Maine, United States. She was the daughter of James A Thorncraft and Mary Thorncraft (born Ketchum), and she married John Hollon Bridges and later Richard Barker. The 1850 US Census shows her in Mars Hill, Aroostook, Maine with husband John H Bridges and children.
findings.claimed_facts: Sarah b. 1816 New Brunswick, d. 1889 Maine; married John Holland/Hollon Bridges (and later Richard Barker); daughter of James A. Thorncraft and Mary Ketchum; lived Mars Hill, Aroostook Co., Maine.
findings.notes: This 'Sarah Barker' (married John Holland Bridges) and worklist person @I322454173001@ 'Sarah F Thorncroft' (same DOB 9 May 1816, same death, same spouse) appear to be the SAME individual — Barker was a second-marriage surname, Thorncroft the maiden name. The Geni record links them: maiden Thorncroft, married Bridges then Barker. The maiden-name parents per Geni are James Thorncraft & Mary Ketchum, which conflicts with worklist @I322454173001@ parents 'Anna Livingston Hutchings; John Bridges' (those appear to be the husband's parents, mis-assigned). Geni page would not load directly; based on search snippets, lower confidence.

findings.search_query: Sarah Thorncroft Bridges 1816 1889 Mars Hill Aroostook Maine James Thorncraft Ketchum
findings.source_title: Sarah Bridges (Thorncroft) (1816 - 1889) - Geni
findings.snippet: Sarah Bridges (Thorncroft), 1816 New Brunswick - 1889 Maine; daughter of James A Thorncraft and Mary (Ketchum) Thorncraft; married John Hollon Bridges and later Richard Barker. James Thorncraft and wife came [to Westfield, Aroostook] in 1839. 1850 census Mars Hill, Aroostook, Maine.
findings.claimed_facts: Sarah F. Thorncroft b. 9 May 1816 New Brunswick, d. 1889 Maine; maiden name Thorncroft/Thorncraft; parents James A. Thorncraft & Mary Ketchum; married John Holland Bridges; resided Aroostook Co., Maine.
findings.notes: Almost certainly the SAME person as worklist @I322454172923@ 'Sarah Barker' (identical 9 May 1816 / d.1889 / spouse John Holland Bridges). CONFLICT with worklist: worklist lists parents 'Anna Livingston Hutchings; John Bridges' but Geni gives parents James Thorncraft & Mary Ketchum (the worklist parents look like the husband's family, mis-attributed). Geni page would not fetch directly; based on search snippets.

findings.search_query: Ann Rathbone 1703 New Shoreham Block Island William Dodge genealogy
findings.source_title: Ann Rathbone (1703-) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Born 9 Aug 1703 New Shoreham, Newport, Rhode Island. Died [date unknown] New Shoreham, Newport, Rhode Island. Spouse William Dodge; married 20 Jun 1728 in New Shoreham, (Block Island) Rhode Island. Daughter of John Rathbone and Ann (Dodge) Rathbone.
findings.claimed_facts: Ann Rathbone b. 9 Aug 1703 New Shoreham, RI; m. William Dodge 20 Jun 1728 New Shoreham; daughter of (Hon.) John Rathbone and Ann (Dodge) Rathbone; children incl. Catherine, Edmund, Benjamin, Nehemiah, John Dodge.
findings.notes: Birth date/place and spouse match worklist exactly. Worklist death 9 May 1787 not confirmed by WikiTree (death date listed unknown there). Adds parents: John Rathbone & Ann Dodge (worklist parents blank).

findings.search_query: Anna Barbara Ritter / Barberie 1700 Montgomery Orange New York German Palatine; Anna Barbara Barberie Montgomery Orange Co NY 1769 died
findings.source_title: (no confident match)
findings.notes: No source confidently matching this person (b.1700 Ennerich/Limburg, d.1769 Montgomery, Orange Co. NY, father John Peter Barberie). Several unrelated 'Anna Barbara Ritter' records exist (e.g., a Stoehr-married one in Zweibruecken). The surname mix Ritter vs father 'Barberie' is itself inconsistent in the worklist. Needs manual research in Montgomery/Orange Co. NY German & Dutch Reformed church records.

findings.search_query: Anna Barbara Weiser Pickard Conrad Nicholas Pickert Schenectady New York genealogy
findings.source_title: Anna Barbara (Weiser) Pickard (1700-1748) | WikiTree; Anna Barbara Weiser Pickard - Find A Grave Memorial 110318626
findings.snippet: Anna Barbara Weiser born in Grossaspach, Duchy of Wuerttemberg [now Biberach, Baden, Germany] to Johann Conrad Weiser and Anna Magdelena Ubelen; one of 15 children. Nicholas married Anna Barbara Weiser about 1722 in Schenectady; they moved to the Mohawk Valley. She was the sister of Colonel Conrad Weiser.
findings.claimed_facts: Anna Barbara Weiser b. 1700 Grossaspach/Aspach, Wuerttemberg (Biberach, Baden); d. 1748; m. Nicholas Pickard (Pickert) ~1722 Schenectady NY; daughter of Johann Conrad Weiser & Anna Magdalena Uebele; sister of Conrad Weiser the Indian agent.
findings.notes: Birth year, birthplace (Aspach/Biberach), parents, and spouse all match worklist. Find a Grave memorial #110318626 exists. Death year 1748 matches worklist 'SEP 1748' (one WikiTree alt title says 1750 — minor conflict).

findings.search_query: Bathsheba Sands Thomas Everitt Sands Point Long Island genealogy
findings.source_title: Bathsheba Everett (Sands) (c.1698 - 1730) - Geni; Long Island Genealogies SANDS FAMILY
findings.snippet: Bathsheba Sands was born at Sands Point, Long Island, daughter of James and Mary (Cornell) Sands, and married Thomas Everett. Baptismal record from Jamaica, Long Island, 24 Jan 1723 shows James Everett as son of Thomas and Bathsheba. James Sands' will drawn 21 Sep 1730 willed 10 pounds to granddaughter Sarah Everett, no mention of Bathsheba, suggesting she was deceased by then.
findings.claimed_facts: Bathsheba Sands b. c.1695-1698 Sands Point, Long Island NY; daughter of James Sands & Mary Cornell; m. Thomas Everett/Everitt; presumed deceased by 21 Sep 1730; descendants moved to PA (Amity, Berks Co.).
findings.notes: Birthplace (Sands Point), spouse (Thomas Everitt/Everett), and ~1695 birth and death by/around 1730 (worklist 21 Sep 1730) all align. Worklist death place 'Lynn, Lehigh, PA' not confirmed; sources point to descendants in Amity/Berks PA. Adds parents James Sands & Mary Cornell (worklist blank).

findings.search_query: Benjamin Ives 1697 Wallingford Connecticut Hannah Moss genealogy
findings.source_title: Person:Benjamin Ives (1) - WereLate; Ives descendant database (brentglad.com)
findings.snippet: Benjamin Ives born November 22, 1697 in Wallingford (some give Meriden), New Haven, Connecticut, died 17 Jan 1754. Married Hannah Moss, daughter of John Moss and Elizabeth Hall, on 6 May 1728 in Wallingford. Children incl. Benjamin (1728/29), Hannah (1732), Lois (1733/34), David (1736).
findings.claimed_facts: Benjamin Ives b. 22 Nov 1697 Wallingford, CT; d. 1754 (17 Jan 1754, some say Goshen, Litchfield Co.); m. Hannah Moss 6 May 1728 Wallingford; parents John Ives Jr. & Mary Gillette per worklist.
findings.notes: Birth date/place and spouse Hannah Moss match worklist exactly. Worklist death 19 Dec 1755 Wallingford conflicts slightly with sources giving 17 Jan 1754 (and possibly Goshen) — minor date/place conflict to verify.

findings.search_query: Conrad Nicholas Pickard 1701 Schenectady baptized Anna Barbara Weiser Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Nicholas Pickard (1701-1776) - Find a Grave Memorial 110318601; Nicholas Pickard (bef.1701-aft.1776) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Nicholas Pickard born 1701 in Schenectady, NY, son of Bartholomew and Eechje (Classez) Pickard, baptized 23 February 1701 at the Dutch Reformed Church in Schenectady. Married Anna Barbara Weiser ~1722 in Schenectady; moved to Mohawk Valley; known for negotiating with the Mohawk. Probably died at Fort Plain; burial unknown.
findings.claimed_facts: Nicholas (Conrad Nicolas) Pickard/Pickert bapt. 23 Feb 1701 Schenectady NY; son of Bartholomew Pickard & Eechje Classez; m. Anna Barbara Weiser ~1722; d. ~1773-1776 (probably Fort Plain, Montgomery Co. NY).
findings.notes: Baptism 23 Feb 1701 Schenectady and spouse Anna Barbara Weiser match worklist. CONFLICT: worklist death '1773'; sources say 'aft.1776' (exact date/place unknown). Find a Grave #110318601. Adds parents Bartholomew Pickard & Eechje Classez (worklist parents blank).

findings.search_query: Deborah Partridge 1717 Preston New London Connecticut Samuel Partridge genealogy
findings.source_title: Preston Connecticut Marriages / Births; Genealogy of New England data pages
findings.snippet: Deborah Partridge born April 28, 1717, daughter of Samuel and Deborah (Rose) Partridge, born Preston, New London, Connecticut, died 1745. On 21 Aug 1733 Deborah Partridge married Isaac Gates; his first wife died 22 May 1745. Sister of Samuel Partridge Jr. (b.1722, m. Ruth Woodward).
findings.claimed_facts: Deborah Partridge b. 28 Apr 1717 Preston, New London Co., CT; daughter of Samuel Partridge & Deborah Rose; m. Isaac Gates 21 Aug 1733; d. 22 May 1745 Preston.
findings.notes: Birth date/place, parents (Samuel Partridge & Deborah Rose), and death 22 May 1745 all match worklist exactly. NEW INFO: spouse was Isaac Gates (worklist spouse field blank).

findings.search_query: Hannah Moss 1709 Wallingford Connecticut Benjamin Ives John Moss genealogy
findings.source_title: Ives/Moss descendant database (brentglad.com); WALLINGFORD Barbour vital records
findings.snippet: Hannah Moss born 11 Nov 1709 in Wallingford, New Haven, Connecticut, daughter of John Moss and Elizabeth Hall. Married Benjamin Ives 6 May 1728 in Wallingford. Hannah died 20 Jan 1787 in Cheshire, New Haven, CT. After Benjamin's death she married Phineas Atwater 15 Jun 1768.
findings.claimed_facts: Hannah Moss b. 11 Nov 1709 Wallingford, CT; d. 20 Jan 1787 Cheshire, CT; m. (1) Benjamin Ives 6 May 1728, (2) Phineas Atwater 1768; daughter of John Moss & Elizabeth Hall.
findings.notes: Birth/death dates and places, and spouse Benjamin Ives, all match worklist exactly. Adds parents John Moss & Elizabeth Hall (worklist blank) and a second marriage to Phineas Atwater.

findings.search_query: Isabella Hamilton 1741 Albemarle Virginia James Little Franklin Georgia genealogy
findings.source_title: Colonel James Little (1737 - 1807) - Geni; James Little (1737-1807) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: James Little married Isabel Hamilton (1741-1821). James Little born 1737 Albemarle County, Virginia, died 1807 Carnesville, Franklin County, Georgia. Scotch-Irish; came to Franklin Co. via VA, NC, SC. Children incl. David (1767), James H (1769), Mary Ann, John William (1775), Ellen (1778).
findings.claimed_facts: Isabella/Isabel Hamilton b. 1741 (Albemarle Co. VA per worklist), d. 1821; wife of (Col.) James Little (1737-1807) of Franklin Co., Georgia.
findings.notes: Birth year 1741, death year 1821, and husband James Little of Franklin Co. GA all align with worklist. Death place Franklin GA matches. Hamilton parents still unconfirmed (worklist blank).

findings.search_query: Johannes Cookeson / Cookson 1654 Kippax Yorkshire Elizabeth Shepheard Lancelot Cookson genealogy
findings.source_title: Lancelot COOKSON, born 1682 (Ancestry index snippet); Cookson WikiTree
findings.snippet: Lancelot Cookson was born in Kippax, Yorkshire, England on 25 June 1682 to Johanis Cookson and Elizabeth Cookson.
findings.claimed_facts: A Johannes/Johanis Cookson of Kippax, Yorkshire had a son Lancelot Cookson b. 25 Jun 1682, wife named Elizabeth — consistent with worklist's Johannes Cookeson (b.1654 Kippax) married to Elizabeth Shepheard and father Lancelott Cookson (here the relationship appears inverted: Lancelot may be Johannes's son, not father).
findings.notes: Only indirect corroboration: a Johanis Cookson of Kippax w/ wife Elizabeth had son Lancelot (b.1682). Worklist lists Lancelott Cookson as Johannes's FATHER, but the source suggests a Lancelot as his SON — possible generational mix-up. Wife surname 'Shepheard' and 1654 birth not directly confirmed. Kippax St Mary parish registers (FreeREG/FamilySearch) needed for verification.

findings.search_query: Josiah Flanders 1691 Salisbury Massachusetts Sarah Prince South Hampton New Hampshire genealogy
findings.source_title: John Flanders (1691-1782) | WikiTree; Lt. John Flanders - Geni
findings.snippet: John Flanders: Born 22 Aug 1691 Salisbury, Essex, Massachusetts Bay. Died 14 Nov 1782 South Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire. Spouse Sarah (Prince) Flanders, married 12 Feb 1715 in Gloucester, Essex, Massachusetts Bay. Son of John Flanders and Elizabeth (Sargent) Flanders.
findings.claimed_facts: The person with worklist's vitals (b. 22 Aug 1691 Salisbury MA, d. 14 Nov 1782 South Hampton NH, m. Sarah Prince) is documented as JOHN Flanders, not Josiah; m. Sarah Prince 12 Feb 1715 Gloucester; parents John Flanders & Elizabeth Sargent.
findings.notes: STRONG CONFLICT on given name: worklist calls him 'Josiah H. Flanders' but every other field (b. 22 Aug 1691 Salisbury, d. 14 Nov 1782 South Hampton NH, wife Sarah Prince) matches JOHN Flanders (WikiTree Flanders-32). A separate real Josiah Flanders existed (b.1700, m. Mehitable Osgood) — the worklist appears to have mislabeled John as Josiah. Also worklist father 'Corp. John Flanders' w/ mother 'Jane Indian Princess Sandusky' is dubious; sources give parents John Flanders & Elizabeth Sargent.

findings.search_query: Rebecca Burge / Rebecca Whitney Joseph Whitney genealogy wife
findings.source_title: Rebecca (Burge) Whitney (1683-aft.1726) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Rebecca Burge (1683-after 1726) wife of Joseph Whitney, married 26 May 1706 in Charlestown, Massachusetts. Mother of Joseph, James, and Daniel Whitney; died after 20 May 1726 in Chelmsford, Middlesex, Massachusetts. Siblings incl. Hannah (Burge) Dutton, Rachel (Burge) Berrett, Susanna (Burge) Chandler, Josiah Burge.
findings.claimed_facts: Rebecca Burge b. 1683, m. Joseph Whitney 26 May 1706 Charlestown MA; d. aft. 20 May 1726 Chelmsford MA; children Joseph, James, Daniel Whitney.
findings.notes: Spouse Joseph Whitney matches worklist; worklist had no dates/places, so this adds b.1683, m.1706 Charlestown, d.aft.1726 Chelmsford. Common name — verify against the specific lineage.

findings.search_query: William Dodge 1707 New Shoreham Block Island Ann Rathbone Nathaniel Dodge genealogy
findings.source_title: Dodge genealogy; descendants of Tristram Dodge (archive.org full text); WikiTree Dodge family
findings.snippet: William Dodge born May 1, 1707, Freeman 1734, died 1786. William Dodge Jr. married 20 Jun 1728, Anne Rathbone (New Shoreham Records); she b. 9 Aug 1703, daughter of John Rathbone and Ann (Dodge) Rathbone. William was son of Nathaniel Dodge (descended from Tristram Dodge). Children: Catherine (1730), Edmund (1733), Benjamin (1735), Nehemiah (1742), John (1747).
findings.claimed_facts: William Dodge Jr. b. 1 May 1707 New Shoreham, RI; d. 1786/1787; son of Nathaniel Dodge; m. Anne Rathbone 20 Jun 1728 New Shoreham; descendant of Tristram Dodge.
findings.notes: Birth date 1 May 1707, birthplace New Shoreham, father Nathaniel Dodge, and spouse Ann Rathbone all match worklist. Death year 1786 vs worklist 9 May 1787 — minor conflict. Confirms parent Nathaniel Dodge (worklist had it as a 'DNA match, not sure' note).

findings.search_query: Samuel Fuller 1658 Plymouth Mercy Eaton Plympton Massachusetts genealogy
findings.source_title: Samuel Fuller III (1659 - 1728) - Geni; Mercy Fuller (Eaton) - Geni; Stedman One Name Study
findings.snippet: Samuel born c.1658 in Plymouth, died 6 September 1728 in Plympton; married Mercy Eaton 7 January 1685/6 in Plymouth. One of the first settlers of Plympton, MA; married Mercy, daughter of Benjamin Eaton. Children incl. Nathaniel (1687), Samuel (1689), Seth (1692), Ebenezer (1695), Benjamin (1696), etc.
findings.claimed_facts: Samuel Fuller b. ~1658 Plymouth, MA; d. 6 Sep 1728 Plympton, MA; m. Mercy Eaton (dau. of Benjamin Eaton) 7 Jan 1685/6 Plymouth; one of first settlers of Plympton; Mayflower descendant line.
findings.notes: Birth ~1658 Plymouth, death 6 Sep 1728 Plympton, and spouse Mercy Eaton all match worklist exactly. Worklist parents (Samuel Fuller & Elizabeth Nichols) consistent with this being Samuel Fuller 'III'. Mayflower (Edward Fuller) descent noted.
